text.title: Claude Code & Cursor at Solo Tier Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding claude-code-and-cursor-at-solo-tier. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST route by task type: Opus for creative / cross-file design + planning; Sonnet for routine refactor and tests; Haiku only for mechanical search / classification.
rule.rationale: Wrong model wastes tokens or produces low-quality output; Opus on a 5-line rename is waste, Sonnet on a system-design call is undershoot.

rule.statement: Repo MUST have CLAUDE.md (or AGENTS.md) at root that names: tech stack, conventions, taboo patterns, where to look first. Without it AI starts every session from zero.
rule.rationale: Convention file is the load-bearing context for repeat sessions; without it the model relearns the codebase each time.

rule.statement: Per task, retrieved context MUST stay ≤2K tokens. Larger contexts dilute attention and produce worse output.
rule.rationale: Above 2K tokens the model's task-fidelity drops; targeted retrieval beats whole-repo dumps.

rule.statement: Non-trivial tasks (≥30 LOC) MUST start with a 1-paragraph spec (problem + constraints + done-criteria) before code is requested.
rule.rationale: Skipping spec produces overshoot ('AI wrote 800 LOC nobody understands'); spec frames the task.

rule.statement: Each iteration MUST have a test (manual or automated) that proves the change works. Trust-only code is forbidden.
rule.rationale: AI-written code without a test pass is a hypothesis, not a working change; production-grade work requires the verification gate.
